# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - staging
  - production

variables:
  APP_NAME: "myapp"
  BUILD_DIR: "build_output"

# Build stage - compile and package application
build_application:
  stage: build
  image: golang:1.21
  script:
    - echo "Building application binaries..."
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/bin
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/configs
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts
    # Build the main binary
    - go build -o ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} ./cmd/main.go
    # Build CLI tool
    - go build -o ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli ./cmd/cli/main.go
    # Copy configuration files
    - cp -r configs/* ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/
    # Copy deployment scripts
    - cp -r deploy/*.sh ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts/
    - chmod +x ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts/*.sh
    # Generate build metadata
    - |
      cat > ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json <<EOF
      {
        "version": "${CI_COMMIT_TAG:-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}}",
        "commit": "${CI_COMMIT_SHA}",
        "branch": "${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}",
        "pipeline_id": "${CI_PIPELINE_ID}",
        "build_date": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
        "built_by": "${GITLAB_USER_LOGIN}"
      }
      EOF
    - echo "Build complete. Artifacts:"
    - find ${BUILD_DIR} -type f
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-build-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      dotenv: build.env
  after_script:
    - echo "APP_VERSION=${CI_COMMIT_TAG:-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}}" > build.env
  tags:
    - docker

# Generate documentation artifacts
build_docs:
  stage: build
  image: python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
    - mkdocs build -d docs_output
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-docs-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - docs_output/
    expire_in: 30 days
  allow_failure: true
  tags:
    - docker

# Test stage - uses build artifacts
unit_tests:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21
  dependencies:
    - build_application
  script:
    - echo "Verifying build artifacts exist..."
    - ls -la ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
    - test -f ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}
    - echo "Running unit tests..."
    - go test -v -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
    - go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-test-results-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - coverage.out
      - coverage.html
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: coverage.out
  tags:
    - docker

integration_tests:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21
  dependencies:
    - build_application
  services:
    - name: postgres:15
      alias: test-db
  variables:
    POSTGRES_DB: test_db
    POSTGRES_USER: test_user
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_pass
    DATABASE_URL: "postgres://test_user:test_pass@test-db:5432/test_db?sslmode=disable"
  script:
    - echo "Running integration tests with built binary..."
    - ls -la ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
    - cat ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json
    # Start the application in background for integration testing
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --config ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/test.yaml &
    - APP_PID=$!
    - sleep 5
    - echo "Running integration test suite..."
    - go test -v -tags=integration ./tests/integration/...
    - kill $APP_PID || true
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-integration-results-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - integration-results/
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: always
  tags:
    - docker

# Smoke test the binary
smoke_test:
  stage: test
  image: alpine:3.18
  dependencies:
    - build_application
  script:
    - echo "Performing smoke tests on artifacts..."
    - test -x ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}
    - test -x ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --version
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli health-check --dry-run
    - echo "Validating configuration files..."
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --validate-config ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/staging.yaml
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --validate-config ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/production.yaml
    - echo "Smoke tests passed."
  tags:
    - docker

# Staging deployment - downloads and executes build artifacts
deploy_staging:
  stage: staging
  image: alpine:3.18
  dependencies:
    - build_application
    - build_docs
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.example.com
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache openssh-client rsync curl jq
    - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
    - echo "${STAGING_SSH_KEY}" | ssh-add -
    - mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    - echo "${STAGING_SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS}" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to staging environment..."
    - cat ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json | jq .
    # Upload artifacts to staging server
    - |
      rsync -avz --delete \
        ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/ \
        deploy@staging.example.com:/opt/${APP_NAME}/bin/
    - |
      rsync -avz \
        ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/staging.yaml \
        deploy@staging.example.com:/opt/${APP_NAME}/config.yaml
    - |
      rsync -avz \
        ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts/ \
        deploy@staging.example.com:/opt/${APP_NAME}/scripts/
    - |
      rsync -avz \
        ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json \
        deploy@staging.example.com:/opt/${APP_NAME}/build-info.json
    # Deploy documentation
    - |
      rsync -avz --delete \
        docs_output/ \
        deploy@staging.example.com:/var/www/docs/
    # Execute deployment script on remote server
    - |
      ssh deploy@staging.example.com << 'DEPLOY_SCRIPT'
        set -e
        cd /opt/${APP_NAME}
        echo "Stopping existing service..."
        sudo systemctl stop ${APP_NAME} || true
        echo "Running database migrations..."
        ./bin/${APP_NAME} migrate --config config.yaml
        echo "Starting service with new binary..."
        sudo systemctl start ${APP_NAME}
        echo "Waiting for service to be healthy..."
        sleep 10
        curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
        echo "Staging deployment complete!"
      DEPLOY_SCRIPT
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-staging-deploy-log-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - staging-deploy.log
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: always
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
  tags:
    - docker

# Staging verification after deployment
verify_staging:
  stage: staging
  image: alpine:3.18
  dependencies:
    - build_application
  needs:
    - job: deploy_staging
      artifacts: false
    - job: build_application
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl jq
    - echo "Verifying staging deployment..."
    # Verify the deployed version matches our build
    - |
      EXPECTED_VERSION=$(cat ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json | jq -r .version)
      DEPLOYED_VERSION=$(curl -sf https://staging.example.com/api/version | jq -r .version)
      if [ "$EXPECTED_VERSION" != "$DEPLOYED_VERSION" ]; then
        echo "Version mismatch! Expected: $EXPECTED_VERSION, Got: $DEPLOYED_VERSION"
        exit 1
      fi
    - echo "Running acceptance tests against staging..."
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli run-acceptance-tests --target https://staging.example.com
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
  tags:
    - docker

# Production deployment - manual gate with artifact passthrough
deploy_production:
  stage: production
  image: alpine:3.18
  dependencies:
    - build_application
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
  when: manual
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache openssh-client rsync curl jq
    - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
    - echo "${PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY}" | ssh-add -
    - mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    - echo "${PRODUCTION_SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS}" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
  script:
    - echo "=== PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT ==="
    - cat ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json | jq .
    # Backup current production artifacts
    - |
      ssh deploy@prod.example.com << 'BACKUP_SCRIPT'
        set -e
        BACKUP_DIR="/opt/${APP_NAME}/backups/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
        mkdir -p $BACKUP_DIR
        cp -r /opt/${APP_NAME}/bin/ $BACKUP_DIR/bin/ || true
        cp /opt/${APP_NAME}/config.yaml $BACKUP_DIR/config.yaml || true
        cp /opt/${APP_NAME}/build-info.json $BACKUP_DIR/build-info.json || true
        echo "Backup created at $BACKUP_DIR"
      BACKUP_SCRIPT
    # Deploy new artifacts to production servers
    - |
      for SERVER in prod-1.example.com prod-2.example.com; do
        echo "Deploying to $SERVER..."
        rsync -avz --delete \
          ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/ \
          deploy@$SERVER:/opt/${APP_NAME}/bin/
        rsync -avz \
          ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/production.yaml \
          deploy@$SERVER:/opt/${APP_NAME}/config.yaml
        rsync -avz \
          ${BUILD_DIR}/scripts/ \
          deploy@$SERVER:/opt/${APP_NAME}/scripts/
        rsync -avz \
          ${BUILD_DIR}/build-info.json \
          deploy@$SERVER:/opt/${APP_NAME}/build-info.json
      done
    # Rolling restart of production servers
    - |
      for SERVER in prod-1.example.com prod-2.example.com; do
        echo "Restarting service on $SERVER..."
        ssh deploy@$SERVER << 'RESTART_SCRIPT'
          set -e
          cd /opt/${APP_NAME}
          echo "Running pre-deploy checks..."
          ./bin/${APP_NAME} pre-deploy-check --config config.yaml
          echo "Running database migrations..."
          ./bin/${APP_NAME} migrate --config config.yaml
          echo "Gracefully restarting service..."
          sudo systemctl reload-or-restart ${APP_NAME}
          sleep 15
          echo "Health check..."
          curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
          echo "Server deployment complete!"
        RESTART_SCRIPT
        echo "$SERVER deployed successfully. Waiting before next server..."
        sleep 30
      done
    - echo "=== PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT COMPLETE ==="
  after_script:
    - |
      # Send deployment notification
      curl -X POST "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "{
          \"text\": \"🚀 Production deployment completed\",
          \"attachments\": [{
            \"color\": \"good\",
            \"fields\": [
              {\"title\": \"Application\", \"value\": \"${APP_NAME}\", \"short\": true},
              {\"title\": \"Version\", \"value\": \"${CI_COMMIT_TAG:-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}}\", \"short\": true},
              {\"title\": \"Deployed by\", \"value\": \"${GITLAB_USER_LOGIN}\", \"short\": true},
              {\"title\": \"Pipeline\", \"value\": \"${CI_PIPELINE_URL}\", \"short\": false}
            ]
          }]
        }" || true
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-production-deploy-log-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - production-deploy.log
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: always
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG
  tags:
    - docker

# Rollback job - uses previously stored artifacts
rollback_production:
  stage: production
  image: alpine:3.18
  when: manual
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
    action: stop
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache openssh-client curl jq
    - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
    - echo "${PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY}" | ssh-add -
    - mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    - echo "${PRODUCTION_SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS}" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
  script:
    - echo "=== PRODUCTION ROLLBACK ==="
    - |
      for SERVER in prod-1.example.com prod-2.example.com; do
        echo "Rolling back on $SERVER..."
        ssh deploy@$SERVER << 'ROLLBACK_SCRIPT'
          set -e
          cd /opt/${APP_NAME}
          LATEST_BACKUP=$(ls -td backups/*/ | head -1)
          echo "Restoring from backup: $LATEST_BACKUP"
          cp -r $LATEST_BACKUP/bin/* bin/
          cp $LATEST_BACKUP/config.yaml config.yaml
          cp $LATEST_BACKUP/build-info.json build-info.json
          sudo systemctl restart ${APP_NAME}
          sleep 10
          curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
          echo "Rollback complete on this server!"
        ROLLBACK_SCRIPT
        sleep 30
      done
    - echo "=== ROLLBACK COMPLETE ==="
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
      when: manual
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG
      when: manual
  tags:
    - docker
